infobox.value: (French: [[ɑ̃ɡjɛ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Fr-BE-Enghien.ogg "File:Fr-BE-Enghien.ogg"); [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Edingen* [[ˈeːdɪŋə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Edingen.ogg "File:Nl-Edingen.ogg"); [Picard](/wiki/Picard_language "Picard language"): *Inguî*; [West Flemish](/wiki/West_Flemish_language "West Flemish language"): *Enge*) is a city and [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") of [Wallonia](/wiki/Wallonia "Wallonia") located in the [province of Hainaut](/wiki/Hainaut_Province "Hainaut Province"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium").

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [50°41′44″N 04°02′28″E﻿ / ﻿50.69556°N 4.04111°E﻿ / 50.69556; 4.04111](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Enghien&params=50_41_44_N_04_02_28_E_region:BE_type:city)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

City in Hainaut Province, Wallonia, Belgium

For the suburb of Paris, France, see [Enghien-les-Bains](/wiki/Enghien-les-Bains "Enghien-les-Bains"). For the title of nobility, see [Duke of Enghien](/wiki/Duke_of_Enghien "Duke of Enghien").

**Enghien** (French: [[ɑ̃ɡjɛ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Fr-BE-Enghien.ogg "File:Fr-BE-Enghien.ogg"); [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Edingen* [[ˈeːdɪŋə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Edingen.ogg "File:Nl-Edingen.ogg"); [Picard](/wiki/Picard_language "Picard language"): *Inguî*; [West Flemish](/wiki/West_Flemish_language "West Flemish language"): *Enge*) is a city and [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") of [Wallonia](/wiki/Wallonia "Wallonia") located in the [province of Hainaut](/wiki/Hainaut_Province "Hainaut Province"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium").

On 1 January 2006, Enghien had a total population of 11,980. The total area is 40.59 km2 (15.67 sq mi), which gives a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 295 inhabitants per km2.

The municipality consists of the following districts: Enghien, [Marcq](/w/index.php?title=Marcq,_Wallonia&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marcq, Wallonia (page does not exist)"), and [Petit-Enghien](/wiki/Petit-Enghien "Petit-Enghien"). It is situated on the Flemish border, and restricted language rights are granted to the Dutch speaking minority (so-called [language facilities](/wiki/Municipalities_with_language_facilities "Municipalities with language facilities")).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Enghien&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Enghien gave its name to a French [duchy](/wiki/Duchy "Duchy") and to the [commune](/wiki/Commune_in_France "Commune in France") of [Enghien-les-Bains](/wiki/Enghien-les-Bains "Enghien-les-Bains"), a suburb of Paris, due to a complex series of family successions: in 1487, [Mary of Luxembourg](/wiki/Marie_de_Luxembourg "Marie de Luxembourg") (d. 1547), the only heir of [Peter II of Luxembourg](/wiki/Peter_II_of_Luxembourg "Peter II of Luxembourg") (d. 1482), [Count](/wiki/Count "Count") of [Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise](/wiki/Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise "Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise") and member of one of the branches of the [House of Luxembourg](/wiki/House_of_Luxembourg "House of Luxembourg"), married [François de Bourbon-Vendôme](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois,_Count_of_Vend%C3%B4me "François, Count of Vendôme") (d. 1495), the great-grandfather of King [Henry IV of France](/wiki/Henry_IV_of_France "Henry IV of France"). Mary of Luxembourg brought as her [dowry](/wiki/Dowry "Dowry") the fief of [Condé-en-Brie](/wiki/Cond%C3%A9-en-Brie "Condé-en-Brie") ([Aisne](/wiki/Aisne "Aisne") *[département](/wiki/D%C3%A9partements_of_France "Départements of France")*, France) and the [county](/wiki/County "County") of Enghien, among others. These fiefs passed to her grandson [Louis I de Bourbon, Prince de Condé](/wiki/Louis_I_de_Bourbon,_Prince_de_Cond%C3%A9 "Louis I de Bourbon, Prince de Condé"), uncle of King Henry IV of France, who started the line of the [Princes of Condé](/wiki/Prince_of_Cond%C3%A9 "Prince of Condé"), the famous cadet branch of the French royal family.

In 1566, the county of Enghien was elevated to a [duchy](/wiki/Duchy "Duchy")-[peerage](/wiki/Peerage_of_France "Peerage of France"). However, the necessary registration process was not completed, so the title became extinct at the death of Louis I de Bourbon in 1569. In 1633, [Henry II, Prince of Condé](/wiki/Henry_II,_Prince_of_Cond%C3%A9 "Henry II, Prince of Condé"), grandson of Louis I de Bourbon, inherited the [duchy of Montmorency](/wiki/Duke_of_Montmorency "Duke of Montmorency"), near Paris, after the execution of [Henri II de Montmorency](/wiki/Henri_II_de_Montmorency "Henri II de Montmorency"), brother of his wife [Charlotte-Marguerite de Montmorency](/wiki/Charlotte-Marguerite_de_Montmorency "Charlotte-Marguerite de Montmorency"). In 1689, King [Louis XIV](/wiki/Louis_XIV "Louis XIV") allowed [Henry III, Prince of Condé](/wiki/Henry_III,_Prince_of_Cond%C3%A9 "Henry III, Prince of Condé"), grandson of Henry II, Prince of Condé, to rename the duchy of Montmorency as "[duchy of Enghien](/wiki/Duke_of_Enghien "Duke of Enghien")", in memory of the duchy of Enghien which the Princes of Condé had lost in 1569 at the death of Louis I de Bourbon.

The city of [Montmorency](/wiki/Montmorency,_Val-d%27Oise "Montmorency, Val-d'Oise"), at the heart of the duchy, continued to be known as "Montmorency", despite the official name change, but the name "Enghien" stuck to the nearby lake and marshland that developed later as a [spa resort](/wiki/Spa_resort "Spa resort") and was incorporated as the [commune](/wiki/Commune_in_France "Commune in France") of [Enghien-les-Bains](/wiki/Enghien-les-Bains "Enghien-les-Bains") in the 19th century.
